title: Abnormal long build time with podman build --layers
body:
Currently experiencing anormal build times on fully cached build:

- docker build: 0m0.696s (reproduce case in github.com/betagouv/mrs )
- podman 1.3.0-dev (full info below) build --layers: 10m59.019s (same repo but podman branch to have the Dockerfile tweaks for podman 1.3.0-dev / master works with podman master)

comments.body: @jpic Any chance you could pull the latest Podman from upstream and try again?   We made a number of changes to containers/storage and containers/image that were included in a later version of Podman that should help with this.
I think he might be on master - 1.3.0-dev sounds like our current upstream dev version
@mheon, I just went with the Buildah Version in info which is 1.8-dev, that should be 1.8.2 or maybe 1.9.-dev for upstream no?
Hm. Did we pull in the wrong tag?

@TomSweeneyRedHat master doesn't seem to help:

first --layer build: 20m57.450s
second --layer build: 16m12.005s

Note that i'm on a 12 CPU server with a load avg that's consistently below 6 with 32GB or RAM of which 30 are available according to `free`, and a neat connection to internet (if you want ssh ask is_null on IRC that's me)
